scenario_results.reason: Refresh intake guidance focused on custody and serial-reconciliation controls without drifting into endpoint operations or reimaging execution.
Stockroom variance analysis tied receiving, inventory, and assignment evidence together while avoiding procurement transaction ownership.
Lease-return review used sanitization and return-evidence guidance to define safe actions and escalation checkpoints.
Warranty exposure was prioritized using business impact and evidence quality rather than supplier selection or negotiation posture.
Repair-versus-replace response stated uncertainty clearly, requested missing lifecycle facts, and stayed out of procurement authority.
Audit traceability output required replayable lineage from receipt through disposition and explicitly identified missing evidence.
Acquisition-inventory review provided phased reconciliation and contract-ambiguity escalation rather than unsupported conclusions.
Unsupported-hardware risk ranking stayed in lifecycle governance and separated infrastructure execution work for orchestrator handoff.
Software entitlement and true-up reasoning were rejected clearly and routed to the correct adjacent specialists.
Endpoint automation build work was refused while preserving the underlying return and reassignment control problem.
Low-confidence handling requested minimum identity, custody, and policy facts instead of inventing a final remediation plan.
Fallback handling preserved the local reviewed baseline and avoided unsupported packaging or commercialization claims when delegated normalization was stale.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against issue #128 requirements, including policy and governance analysis, commercial and lifecycle reasoning, evidence and traceability quality, risk-based prioritization, low-confidence escalation, out-of-scope rejection, and shared-meta-agent fallback behavior.
No unreviewed regressions remain and human verification is recorded in readiness evidence.
